title: CI: add nightly cron runs
body:
With cirrus we had had nightly cron runs on main an older branches to verify that all still works.

IMO testing main nightly on an active repo like this likely offer little to no value? At least until we actually move some tests to run nightly only.

For the N-1 release branch this would make sense but the gh actions schedule trigger seems to have a major limit, it only runs on the default branch. We likely need to find a work around if we want that.

https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/events-that-trigger-workflows#schedule

comments.body:
Given the GHA limitation you mentioned, the only workaround I found was to invoke the main ci.yml (as a callable workflow) from, let's say, a nightly.yml workflow, and have the main workflow check out the input ref passed through. This also means some of the existing jobs in ci.yml (validate-source, path-filter, etc.) would need to be gated, as not all of them make sense for a nightly test run. 

And yes, I don't really see a huge advantage over running the nightly tests on main, given how high PR traffic normally is.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
